specificity.note: Names the exact node (cust.operator-large), the 0.40 spend share, specific accounts (Equinix, Digital Realty, Vertiv's major-customer list), the instrument (global AVLs/framework agreements), and the 12-month reachability window. The perturbation is a concrete 'exclude' op, not a vague haircut.

nonObviousness.note: Goes beyond 'the 0.40 band could be wide' by naming a NEW reason — reachability via HQ-negotiated frameworks, which the band structurally cannot express ('the share may be exactly 0.40 and still be zero for a regional entrant'). Docked one point because AVL gating at hyperscalers is reasonably known to founders selling into data-center infrastructure.

mechanismDepth.note: All three hops carry refs and each is independently plausible: global AVL procurement → no channel evidence behind the 0.40 → first-buyer strategy targets a foreclosed cell. The propagation step is partly epistemic ('the model cannot distinguish') rather than causal, and the rationale itself concedes the mechanism 'weakens across the segment' below the apex accounts.

evidenceQuality.note: Key premise (framework procurement at top accounts) has ≥2 independent corroborations (Vertiv customer list, Generac global supply agreement, Google/Achilles prequalification), and the risk honestly flags contested status with contradicting Greenergy and European-bank-switch cases plus a downward likelihood revision. Falls short of 4 because the contradicting evidence directly undercuts uniform foreclosure across the segment the model bundles together.

decisionRelevance.note: Directly challenges the declared first-buyer GTM and Year-1 pipeline, with mitigations (procurement interviews probing local vs HQ sourcing, parallel OEM/white-label route) executable this quarter before GTM spend commits. Both indicators name concrete monitorable venues (supplier portals, TED tenders, Vertiv/Schneider earnings calls) with thresholds.
